learn.title: Dividing by 10
learn.content: When you divide a multiple of 10 by 10, just drop the zero. 70 ÷ 10 = 7. Think: 10 groups of 7 = 70, so 70 ÷ 10 = 7.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.2 'Divide by 10' pp.269-272
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Use ×10 Facts
learn.content: If 10 × 6 = 60, then 60 ÷ 10 = 6. Use your multiplication table of 10 in reverse. Think: 10 × ? = 60 → ? = 6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.2 'Divide by 10' pp.269-272
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Skip Count Back by 10
learn.content: Count back by 10s from 50: 50, 40, 30, 20, 10, 0 — 5 jumps. So 50 ÷ 10 = 5. Each jump back represents one group of 10.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.2 'Divide by 10' pp.269-272
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Number Line: Divide by 10
learn.content: For 40 ÷ 10: start at 40, make jumps of 10 back to 0. Count: 40→30→20→10→0 — that's 4 jumps. So 40 ÷ 10 = 4. Each jump = one group of 10.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.2 'Divide by 10' pp.269-272
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Drop-the-Zero Rule
learn.content: For any multiple of 10: n×10 ÷ 10 = n. Just remove the trailing zero. 10÷10=1, 20÷10=2, 30÷10=3, ... 90÷10=9, 100÷10=10. This works because 10 groups of n = 10n, so 10n÷10 = n.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.2 'Divide by 10' pp.269-272
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 0 떼기 루틴 — ÷10은 끝 0 떼기
learn.content: 10의 배수를 10으로 나누면 끝의 0 한 개를 떼면 답. 예: 30 ÷ 10 = 3 (30에서 0 떼면 3). 예: 80 ÷ 10 = 8, 100 ÷ 10 = 10. 왜 작동? 30 = 3 × 10이니까 30 ÷ 10 = 3. 단계 1) 끝자리가 0인지 확인 (이번 단원은 모두 0). 단계 2) 끝 0 한 개만 떼면 답. 흔한 실수 (M01): 0을 안 떼고 30이라 답하거나 두 개 떼서 0이라 답.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.2 'Divide by 10' pp.269-272
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.2 'Divide by 10' pp.269-272
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 자릿값 검산 — 답 × 10 = 원래 수?
learn.content: ÷10 답을 구한 뒤 검산: 답 × 10 = 원래 수? 예: 70 ÷ 10 = 7 → 검산: 7 × 10 = 70 ✓. 예: 100 ÷ 10 = 10 → 검산: 10 × 10 = 100 ✓. 🔍 검증: 답 한 자리(또는 두 자리) → 곱 두 자리(또는 세 자리)인지 확인. 흔한 실수 (M01): 60 ÷ 10 = 60(0 안 뗌) 또는 = 0(두 자리 다 뗌) — 정답은 끝 0 정확히 한 개만 떼는 것.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 604 − 247.
practice_r2.explanation: 604−247: 4−7 borrow → 14−7=7; tens 9−4=5 (after lending across zero); hundreds 5−2=3. Result: 357.
